And so if I go back to the Wiki Data page,
Wiki Data has something we call a schema. And the schema is basically like, what are the
relations that are included in the database? So one of the relations that's included in the databases
instance of, I guess, also image, lots of images, signature, sex or gender, country of citizenship.
And these relations are like decided a priori by the people who created Wiki Data. And there's lots and
lots of them, but that doesn't necessarily mean that like similarly to the problem of not having

in Wiki Data, but I think it's not in Wiki Data. So as you go down to more esoteric concepts or
specialized domains or stuff like that, you're almost always guaranteed to not have all the entities
you need or not have all of the relations you need. So that's the problem that schema free
extraction is trying to solve. It's trying to figure out how we can jointly figure out the schema
together with the information that you want to extract.
And the most famous example of this is something called open information extraction.
An open information extraction, basically what it's saying is we don't need a schema.
There's no there's no schema. The only schema that we have is the actual text in the sentences
that we're referring to the entities. So if we have united, united has a hub in Chicago,
which is the headquarters of United Continental Holdings. The relation is literally has a hub in.
That's the relation. And then for this we have Chicago is the headquarters of.
But the problem with this is that this cannot abstract away. So if we had another sentence that said
Chicago or United Continental Holdings has its headquarters in Chicago, that would be treated
as completely different. You wouldn't be able to like group those two together.
So in open information extraction, actually a lot of the methods, this is one of the few things
where people still use rule-based systems as kind of like almost state-of-the-art systems.
But basically the reason why you're able to do this is it's not actually that hard to extract
kind of the relevant strings between two entities. And so both the precision and recall are pretty high.
And another reason why people use rule-based systems is because they like you want to run it over

model. So some examples of this include text runner and reverb. The basic ideas behind them
is that you use a parser to extract to do a syntactic analysis of the sentence in extract
according to rules. So for example the relation must contain a predicate. The subject and object
must be noun phrases other things like this. And then what they did later is what they did in this

an even faster model to extract over large amounts of data. So they basically use this as a
